zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Basset of Sapcote於1134年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾薩普可特時，他的父親Richard Basset Chief Justice of England44歲，母親Matilda Ridel30歲。他和de Stafford至少有2個兒子。他去世於1185年在英國萊希斯特斯爾享年51歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Basset
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from a diminutive of Old French bas(se) ‘short, of low stature’ (see Bas ).
English: variant of   Bassett  , a cognate of 1 above.
In some cases also an altered form of French Besset (see Bessette 2). Compare Bassette 1.

variants.name: Bas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Mauricio, Abelardo, Florencio, Francisco, Jorge, Luis, Manuel, Pedro, Soledad, Vicente.  Eastern Slavic Dmytro, Liya, Pyotr, Sergey.
Catalan: habitational name from a place so named in the district of La Garrotxa, Catalonia.
Basque: topographic name for someone who lived by a wood, from baso ‘wood, forest’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Basset of Sapcote was born in 1134, in Sapcote, Leicestershire, England, his father, Richard Basset Chief Justice of England, was 44 and his mother, Matilda Ridel, was 30. He had at least 2 sons with de Stafford. He died in 1185, in Leicestershire, England, at the age of 51.

Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec de Stafford. Il est décédé en 1185, à Leicestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 51 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William de Basset of Sapcote(이)가 1134년 영국 레이스스터실르 사p컷에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Basset Chief Justice of England(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Ridel(은)는 30세였다. 그는 de Stafford(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1185년 영국 레이스스터실르에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Basset of Sapcote родился в 1134 г. в местности сапкот, лисестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Basset Chief Justice of England было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Matilda Ridel, 30.
